DOCUMENTS.title: Python List Comprehensions
DOCUMENTS.content: List comprehensions in Python provide a concise way to create lists using a single line of code with optional filtering.

DOCUMENTS.title: Python Decorators
DOCUMENTS.content: Decorators in Python are functions that wrap another function to extend its behavior without modifying its source code.

DOCUMENTS.title: Python Generators
DOCUMENTS.content: Generators use the yield keyword to return values lazily, making them memory-efficient for large data processing.

DOCUMENTS.title: Python Context Managers
DOCUMENTS.content: Context managers using the with statement handle resource setup and teardown automatically, commonly used for file I/O and DB connections.

DOCUMENTS.title: Python Type Hints
DOCUMENTS.content: Type hints in Python allow static type checking using annotations like str, int, list[str], and Optional from the typing module.

DOCUMENTS.title: Python Virtual Environments
DOCUMENTS.content: Virtual environments isolate Python project dependencies so different projects can use different package versions without conflict.

DOCUMENTS.title: Python Exception Handling
DOCUMENTS.content: Python uses try, except, else, and finally blocks to handle exceptions gracefully and prevent program crashes.

DOCUMENTS.title: Python Dataclasses
DOCUMENTS.content: Dataclasses automatically generate __init__, __repr__, and __eq__ methods, reducing boilerplate for classes that primarily store data.

DOCUMENTS.title: Python Async Await
DOCUMENTS.content: Async and await keywords in Python enable writing non-blocking code that can handle many concurrent tasks without threads.

DOCUMENTS.title: Python Lambda Functions
DOCUMENTS.content: Lambda functions are anonymous single-expression functions useful for short operations passed as arguments to map, filter, or sorted.

DOCUMENTS.title: MongoDB Aggregation Pipeline
DOCUMENTS.content: MongoDB aggregation pipelines process documents through stages like $match, $group, $sort, and $project to transform and analyze data.

DOCUMENTS.title: MongoDB Indexes
DOCUMENTS.content: Indexes in MongoDB improve query performance by allowing the database to quickly locate documents without scanning the entire collection.

DOCUMENTS.title: MongoDB Schema Design
DOCUMENTS.content: MongoDB schema design involves choosing between embedding related data in one document or referencing it across multiple collections.

DOCUMENTS.title: MongoDB Transactions
DOCUMENTS.content: Multi-document ACID transactions in MongoDB allow multiple read and write operations to succeed or fail as a single atomic unit.

DOCUMENTS.title: MongoDB Change Streams
DOCUMENTS.content: Change streams allow applications to subscribe to real-time changes in a collection, database, or entire MongoDB deployment.

DOCUMENTS.title: MongoDB Atlas Search
DOCUMENTS.content: Atlas Search provides full-text search capabilities built on Apache Lucene, supporting fuzzy matching, facets, and relevance scoring.

DOCUMENTS.title: MongoDB Sharding
DOCUMENTS.content: Sharding in MongoDB horizontally distributes data across multiple servers using a shard key to handle large datasets and high throughput.

DOCUMENTS.title: MongoDB Replica Sets
DOCUMENTS.content: Replica sets provide high availability by maintaining multiple copies of data across servers with automatic failover.

DOCUMENTS.title: MongoDB GridFS
DOCUMENTS.content: GridFS is a specification for storing large files in MongoDB by splitting them into chunks and storing metadata separately.

DOCUMENTS.title: MongoDB TTL Indexes
DOCUMENTS.content: TTL indexes automatically delete documents after a specified time period, useful for expiring sessions, logs, or temporary data.

DOCUMENTS.title: What is a Vector Embedding
DOCUMENTS.content: A vector embedding is a numerical representation of data like text or images as a fixed-size array of floats that captures semantic meaning.

DOCUMENTS.title: Cosine Similarity
DOCUMENTS.content: Cosine similarity measures the angle between two vectors in high-dimensional space, returning a score between 0 and 1 for semantic closeness.

DOCUMENTS.title: HNSW Algorithm
DOCUMENTS.content: HNSW is a graph-based approximate nearest neighbor algorithm that builds a layered structure for fast and accurate vector search.

DOCUMENTS.title: Semantic Search vs Keyword Search
DOCUMENTS.content: Semantic search finds results based on meaning and context, while keyword search matches exact words or patterns in documents.

DOCUMENTS.title: RAG Architecture
DOCUMENTS.content: Retrieval-augmented generation combines vector search to fetch relevant context from a knowledge base before passing it to an LLM for grounded answers.

DOCUMENTS.title: Vector Search Indexing
DOCUMENTS.content: Vector search indexes precompute relationships between embeddings using algorithms like HNSW or IVF to enable fast approximate nearest neighbor queries.

DOCUMENTS.title: Sentence Transformers
DOCUMENTS.content: Sentence transformers are BERT-based models fine-tuned to produce semantically meaningful sentence embeddings for tasks like similarity and clustering.

DOCUMENTS.title: Chunking Strategies for RAG
DOCUMENTS.content: Chunking splits large documents into smaller overlapping segments before embedding to improve retrieval precision in RAG pipelines.

DOCUMENTS.title: Hybrid Search
DOCUMENTS.content: Hybrid search combines dense vector search with sparse keyword search using reciprocal rank fusion to improve overall retrieval quality.

DOCUMENTS.title: Vector Database Comparison
DOCUMENTS.content: Pinecone, Qdrant, Weaviate, and MongoDB Atlas Vector Search differ in hosting model, filtering support, and scalability characteristics.

DOCUMENTS.title: CAP Theorem
DOCUMENTS.content: The CAP theorem states that a distributed system can only guarantee two of three properties: consistency, availability, and partition tolerance.

DOCUMENTS.title: Database Sharding Strategies
DOCUMENTS.content: Sharding strategies include range-based, hash-based, and directory-based approaches to distribute data across database nodes.

DOCUMENTS.title: Rate Limiting Algorithms
DOCUMENTS.content: Common rate limiting algorithms include token bucket, leaky bucket, fixed window, and sliding window, each with different burst handling characteristics.

DOCUMENTS.title: Message Queue Patterns
DOCUMENTS.content: Message queues like RabbitMQ and Kafka decouple producers and consumers, enabling async processing, load leveling, and fault tolerance.

DOCUMENTS.title: Caching Strategies
DOCUMENTS.content: Caching strategies include cache-aside, write-through, write-behind, and read-through, each with different consistency and performance trade-offs.

DOCUMENTS.title: API Gateway Pattern
DOCUMENTS.content: An API gateway acts as a single entry point for clients, handling routing, authentication, rate limiting, and request transformation.

DOCUMENTS.title: Circuit Breaker Pattern
DOCUMENTS.content: The circuit breaker pattern prevents cascading failures by stopping calls to a failing service and allowing it time to recover.

DOCUMENTS.title: Saga Pattern
DOCUMENTS.content: The Saga pattern manages distributed transactions across microservices using a sequence of local transactions with compensating rollback steps.

DOCUMENTS.title: CQRS Pattern
DOCUMENTS.content: CQRS separates read and write models into distinct services, optimizing each independently for query performance and write throughput.

DOCUMENTS.title: Event Sourcing
DOCUMENTS.content: Event sourcing stores state changes as an immutable sequence of events rather than current state, enabling full audit trails and time travel queries.

DOCUMENTS.title: Supervised vs Unsupervised Learning
DOCUMENTS.content: Supervised learning trains on labeled data to predict outputs, while unsupervised learning finds hidden patterns in unlabeled data.

DOCUMENTS.title: Overfitting and Regularization
DOCUMENTS.content: Overfitting occurs when a model memorizes training data. Regularization techniques like L1, L2, and dropout reduce overfitting by penalizing complexity.

DOCUMENTS.title: Transformer Architecture
DOCUMENTS.content: Transformers use self-attention mechanisms to process sequences in parallel, enabling models like BERT and GPT to understand long-range dependencies.

DOCUMENTS.title: Fine-tuning LLMs
DOCUMENTS.content: Fine-tuning adapts a pretrained language model to a specific task or domain by continuing training on a smaller labeled dataset.

DOCUMENTS.title: Attention Mechanism
DOCUMENTS.content: Attention allows models to dynamically focus on relevant parts of the input sequence when producing each output token, enabling context-aware predictions.

DOCUMENTS.title: Embeddings in NLP
DOCUMENTS.content: Word and sentence embeddings map tokens to dense vectors in a continuous space where semantically similar words cluster together.

DOCUMENTS.title: Transfer Learning
DOCUMENTS.content: Transfer learning reuses a model pretrained on a large dataset as the starting point for a new task, reducing training time and data requirements.

DOCUMENTS.title: Gradient Descent
DOCUMENTS.content: Gradient descent iteratively adjusts model weights in the direction that minimizes the loss function, with variants like Adam and SGD for different scenarios.

DOCUMENTS.title: Tokenization in LLMs
DOCUMENTS.content: Tokenization splits raw text into subword units using algorithms like BPE or WordPiece before converting them to integer IDs for model input.

DOCUMENTS.title: Vector Quantization
DOCUMENTS.content: Vector quantization compresses embeddings by mapping them to the nearest entry in a learned codebook, reducing storage while preserving semantic structure.

QUERIES.query: how do I avoid writing repetitive boilerplate in Python classes
run multiple tasks at the same time in Python
save memory when processing large files in Python
automatically remove old records after some time
listen to database changes in real time
store files and images in MongoDB
find similar documents based on meaning not keywords
how does vector search stay fast with millions of records
ground an LLM with my own private data
prevent one failing service from taking down the whole system
handle distributed transactions across multiple microservices
separate reading and writing for better performance
reuse a pretrained model for a new task
model performing well on training but badly on new data
how does a model decide what part of input to focus on
